title: HYPRDeck Control
description: A comprehensive and robust solution for controlling and monitoring Blackmagic Design HyperDeck disk recorders directly from Unreal Engine.

sections.title: Overview
body: The HYPRDeck Control plugin provides a comprehensive and robust solution for controlling and monitoring Blackmagic Design HyperDeck disk recorders directly from within Unreal Engine. By leveraging the HyperDeck's REST API and real-time WebSocket interface, this plugin allows for seamless integration of professional recording hardware into your virtual production pipeline.

Fully exposed to Blueprints, you can easily create custom control UIs, automate complex recording workflows, and build dynamic, media-driven experiences  -  almost all necessary functions are exposed as Blueprint Nodes.

sections.title: Features
body: Full Blueprint Transport Control - Programmatically start/stop recordings, initiate playback, pause, set variable playback speeds (including reverse), and stop the transport.
Advanced Timeline Navigation - Seek to specific frames or use the ClipNext and ClipPrevious nodes to jump between clips in the active timeline.

Real-Time Event System - The plugin listens for state changes via WebSockets, providing instant bindable events in Blueprints for: transport mode changes, active clip index changes during playback, disk list updates (e.g. when an SSD is inserted or removed), and timeline content changes.

State & Media Querying - Access up-to-date information about the device including connection status, transport info (mode, speed, timecode), and detailed lists of all media disks and clips they contain.
Remote Configuration - Configure device settings directly from Unreal Engine such as recording codec, video format, and active video input source.

sections.title: Initial Setup
steps: To use the plugin in your project, first get a reference to its Engine Subsystem in your Blueprint (e.g., in a Widget Blueprint, Player Controller, or Level Blueprint).
In the Event Graph of your Blueprint, on Event Begin Play (for actors) or Event Construct (for widgets), add a Get Subsystem node.
In the Subsystem Class dropdown, select HyperDeck Engine Subsystem.
Right-click the Return Value pin and select Promote to variable. Name it something clear like HyperDeckSubsystem. This gives you a reusable reference throughout the Blueprint.

sections.caption: Getting the HyperDeck Engine Subsystem reference in Blueprint

sections.title: 1. Connecting to the HyperDeck
steps: Drag your HyperDeckSubsystem variable into the graph and pull a wire from it.
Call the Connect function, providing the IP address of your HyperDeck.
To react when the connection status changes, bind to the On Connection Changed event.

sections.caption: Connecting to the HyperDeck and binding to connection events

sections.title: 2. Controlling the Transport
body: Once connected, use the transport functions:
Start Recording - Begins a new recording. You can optionally provide a clip name.
Start Playback - Puts the device in Output mode and begins playing the timeline at normal speed.
Stop Transport - Stops any current recording or playback.
Set Playback Speed - Changes the playback speed. 1.0 is normal, 0.0 is pause, and negative values play in reverse.

sections.title: 3. Reacting to Real-Time Events
steps: Select your HyperDeckSubsystem variable in the My Blueprint panel.
In the Details panel, you will see a list of all available events (e.g., On Transport Mode Changed, On Disk List Updated).
Click the green + button next to an event to create a corresponding event node in your Event Graph.
This event node will now execute automatically whenever that specific state changes on the HyperDeck, no matter what caused the change.

sections.title: 4. Querying Information
body: You can get the current state of the device at any time using the pure (blue) nodes:
Is Connected - Returns a boolean for the WebSocket connection status.
Get Last Transport Info - Returns the FHyperDeckTransportInfo struct containing the current mode, speed, timecode, and clip index.
Get Cached Disk List - Returns an array of FHyperDeckDiskInfo structs, perfect for populating a UI list.
Get Active Disk Info - Returns the FHyperDeckDiskInfo for only the currently active disk.

sections.title: HYPRDeck Remote UI Widget
body: Click the red settings icon in the editor toolbar to launch the HYPRDeck Remote UI. The widget provides a complete visual control surface for the HyperDeck, driven entirely by the Blueprint API described above.
The UI is divided into four main sections:

sections.title: Widget  -  Connection Status
body: This section indicates the connection state of the plugin to the HyperDeck.

Wi-Fi Icon - Displays the current connection status. Green means the plugin is successfully connected to the HyperDeck's WebSocket for real-time events (updated by binding to OnConnectionChanged). Gray means disconnected.
Spinner Icon - Appears while the plugin is attempting to connect, providing visual feedback for a pending state.

sections.title: Widget  -  Input & Mode Selection
body: This group of buttons configures the HyperDeck's primary mode and input source.

SDI / HDMI - Select the active video input. Clicking calls SetVideoInput on the HyperDeck Subsystem. The currently active input is highlighted blue, updated in real-time by binding to OnInputVideoSourceChanged.

OUT / IN - Set the primary transport mode. IN (Input Preview) shows the live video input - calls SetTransportMode with InputPreview. OUT (Output) shows playback from the media disks - calls SetTransportMode with Output. Active mode is highlighted blue, driven by OnTransportModeChanged.

sections.title: Widget  -  Transport & Playback Controls
body: This section provides a standard set of controls for recording and playback.
Record (●) - Starts a new recording. Calls StartRecording. The button turns red or flashes when the transport mode changes to InputRecord.
Stop (■) - Stops the current recording or playback. Calls StopTransport.
Play (►) - Starts playback of the timeline. Calls StartPlayback.
Previous Clip (|◄) - Jumps to the beginning of the previous clip. Calls ClipPrevious.
Next Clip (►|) - Jumps to the beginning of the next clip. Calls ClipNext.
Rewind (◄◄) - Plays in reverse at fast speed. Calls SetPlaybackSpeed with a negative value (e.g., -2.0).
Fast Forward (►►) - Plays forward at fast speed. Calls SetPlaybackSpeed with a value greater than 1.0 (e.g., 2.0).
Film Strip Icon - Refresh button.

sections.title: Widget  -  Media Status
body: This bottom row provides a real-time visualization of the media disks connected to the HyperDeck. The entire section is populated and updated by binding to the OnDiskListUpdated event.
Disk Icons (ssd1, ssd2) - Each icon represents a physical media slot. The icon shows an SSD when a disk is detected.
Green Name - Indicates this is the active disk for recording and playback (where bIsActive is true).
Blue Bar - Capacity meter showing how full the disk is, calculated from TotalSpace and RemainingSpace in the FHyperDeckDiskInfo struct.
"Not Mounted" - No media detected in that slot. Different icons represent different slot types (e.g., SD card, USB-C).

sections.caption: HYPRDeck Remote UI  -  full component breakdown

sections.title: Component  -  Device Connection & Identification
body: Connect to device (Wi-Fi Icon) - Initiates the connection to the HyperDeck using its configured IP address. Calls the Connect function. The icon turns green on successful connection, driven by the OnConnectionChanged event.

Identify Device (Flashing Light Icon) - Triggers the HyperDeck's identify feature, causing lights on the physical hardware to flash for easy identification. Calls IdentifyDevice with true. The feature deactivates automatically after a few seconds.

sections.title: Component  -  Input & Mode Selection
body: Switch to SDI / Switch to HDMI - Selects the active video input source for recording and preview. Calls SetVideoInput, passing either SDI or HDMI. The currently active input is highlighted, updated by binding to OnInputVideoSourceChanged.

Input Preview (IN Button) - Sets the device to display the live video signal from the selected input. Calls SetTransportMode with the InputPreview value. Button is highlighted when in Input Preview mode, updated by OnTransportModeChanged.

Output Preview (OUT Button) - Sets the device to display the video signal from media playback. Calls SetTransportMode with the Output value. Button is highlighted when in Output mode, updated by OnTransportModeChanged.

sections.title: Component  -  Recording & Timeline Navigation
body: Start Recording (● Icon) - Begins a new recording on the active disk. Calls StartRecording. The icon turns red when OnTransportModeChanged reports the mode is InputRecord.
Play Previous Clip (|◄ Icon) - Seeks to the beginning of the previous clip in the timeline. Calls ClipPrevious.
Play Next Clip (►| Icon) - Seeks to the beginning of the next clip in the timeline. Calls ClipNext.

sections.title: Component  -  Playback Controls
body: Play Clip (► Icon) - Starts playback of the current timeline from the current position. Calls StartPlayback.
Stop (■ Icon) - Stops any active recording or playback session. Calls StopTransport. Note: this is a universal stop command, not specific to recording.
Increase Play Speed (►► Icon) - Increases forward playback speed (e.g., to 2.0x, 4.0x). Calls SetPlaybackSpeed with a value greater than 1.0.
Decrease Play Speed (◄◄ Icon) - Decreases playback speed, entering slow-motion or reverse playback. Calls SetPlaybackSpeed with a value less than 1.0 (e.g., 0.5 for slow-motion or -1.0 for reverse).

sections.title: Component  -  Media Status Display
body: This section provides a real-time overview of all connected media slots. The entire display is driven by data received from the OnDiskListUpdated event.
Media Slots (SSD Slot 1, Card Slot 1, etc.) - Each of the five slots represents a physical media interface on the HyperDeck. The UI displays an icon when a disk is detected and mounted.

Green Means Active - The name of a disk (e.g., "ssd1") is highlighted in green when it is the currently active disk for recording and playback. Determined by the bIsActive property in the FHyperDeckDiskInfo struct.
Filled Space (Blue Bar) - This progress bar visualizes used disk capacity, calculated from TotalSpace and RemainingSpace in the FHyperDeckDiskInfo struct.
